original_role: Prevented boundary/transition contamination of robustness verdicts by excluding edge-regime temperatures and high-T non-switching rows.
canonical_equivalent: No complete replacement in current robustness script; contract requires explicit canonical admissibility filters (exclude 4K,30K, force-remove 34K, plus high-T width/peak/amplitude guards).
failure_if_removed: Boundary-driven instability re-enters acceptance metrics, producing non-physical sensitivity and invalid comparability across temperatures.

original_role: Restricted robustness perturbations to canonical-equivalent definitions so tests measure estimator sensitivity rather than observable redefinition.
canonical_equivalent: Canonical contract retains this as allowed/rejected perturbation classes for acceptance.
failure_if_removed: Non-equivalent methods dominate variance and conclusions become about definition changes, not canonical robustness.

original_role: Ensured robustness used a single trusted source and avoided legacy/missing-run contamination.
canonical_equivalent: Replaced by canonical trust-lock requirement: inputs must come from TRUSTED_CANONICAL classification; legacy sources forbidden.
failure_if_removed: Input mismatch and non-reproducible provenance; canonical_input_confirmed fails and run validity cannot be established.

original_role: Kept collapse/normalization coordinate fixed across variants so robustness metrics reflected parameter perturbation only.
canonical_equivalent: Replaced by canonical function-space requirement (no coordinate-sweep in primary acceptance; fixed coordinate only for optional auxiliary diagnostics).
failure_if_removed: Variant-dependent coordinates confound metrics; instability cannot be attributed to the intended perturbation class.
